_comment: CHARACTER-MODEL REGISTRY — animated third-person player-character models. DELIBERATELY separate from props.json / fixtures.json: those catalogs feed the collider-baking + measured-bounds physics pipeline, which a player character must NEVER pass through (it would grow a physics collider it shouldn't have). Nothing here is disguisable or collidable — these are pure view models drawn over the networked player capsule. Loaded by js/config.js into cfg.characterModels and consumed only by js/scene.js. Both GLBs are CC0 by Quaternius (via poly.pizza).

hunter._comment: SWAT soldier shown to OTHER players for a REMOTE hunter (the local hunter stays first-person and never renders this). Clips are 'CharacterArmature|<name>' in the GLB — matched by SUFFIX in scene.js, so the prefix here is advisory only.
hunter._yawComment: yawOffsetDeg rotates the model about vertical so its FRONT faces the networked facing yaw. Set to 180 to fix the 'faces backwards' symptom VRmike saw in live 2-player testing (the soldier's native forward is +Z; the game's is -Z). HOT-TUNABLE: if a live browser shows it still off (running sideways etc.), nudge this — no rebuild.

anim._comment: moveThreshold (u/s): below this the hunter plays idle. refSpeed (u/s): the speed at which run clips play at timeScale 1 — matches rules.moveSpeed (6). timeScale is clamped to [minTimeScale,maxTimeScale]. crossfadeSeconds: blend time between clips (no popping).

weapon._comment: Assault rifle parented to the right-wrist bone. position (bone-local units), rotationDeg (bone-local degrees) and scale are HOT-TUNABLE grip nudges — Quaternius gun + Quaternius character align close out of the box, so these default to a light nudge; adjust here (no rebuild) if the grip looks off in a live browser. attachBone must match a bone name in the GLB (Wrist.R / Wrist.L exist).
